content.problemStatement:
You are given a matrix AAA of size N×MN \times MN×M.

The height of the cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) is represented by a non-negative integer A(i,j)A_{(i, j)}A(i,j)​.

Water can only flow from a cell to an adjacent cell (left, right, up, and down) if the height of the adjacent cell is **equal to or lower** than the current cell.

There is a red lake that touches the right and bottom border of the matrix and a blue lake that touches the left and top border of the matrix. Both lakes are at **zero** height.

Find the number of cells in the matrix from which water can flow to both red and blue lakes.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of rows and columns of the matrix, respectively.
The next NNN lines describe the rows. The ithi^{th}ith of these MMM lines contain MMM space-separated integers, denoting the ithi^{th}ith column.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of cells in the matrix from which water can flow to both red and blue lakes.
constraints: Sum of N×MN\times MN×M over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1062\cdot 10^62⋅106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Water from cells A(1,2)A_{(1, 2)}A(1,2)​ and A(2,1)A_{(2, 1)}A(2,1)​ can flow to both red and blue lakes. These two cells are directly attached to both lakes.
Test case 222: Water from cells A(1,1),A(1,2)A_{(1, 1)}, A_{(1, 2)}A(1,1)​,A(1,2)​ and A(1,3)A_{(1, 3)}A(1,3)​ can flow to both red and blue lakes. These three cells are directly attached to both lakes.
Test case 333: Water from cells A(1,3),A(2,1),A(2,2)A_{(1, 3)}, A_{(2, 1)}, A_{(2, 2)}A(1,3)​,A(2,1)​,A(2,2)​ and A(2,3)A_{(2, 3)}A(2,3)​ can flow to both red and blue lakes.

A(1,3)A_{(1, 3)}A(1,3)​: Directly attached to red lake. Possible path to blue lake: (1,3)→(1,2)→(1,1)→(1, 3) \rightarrow (1, 2) \rightarrow (1, 1) \rightarrow(1,3)→(1,2)→(1,1)→ blue lake.
A(2,1)A_{(2, 1)}A(2,1)​: Directly attached to both lakes.
A(2,2)A_{(2, 2)}A(2,2)​: Directly attached to red lake. Possible path to blue lake: (2,2)→(2,1)→(2, 2) \rightarrow (2, 1) \rightarrow(2,2)→(2,1)→ blue lake.
A(2,3)A_{(2, 3)}A(2,3)​: Directly attached to red lake. Possible path to blue lake: (2,3)→(2,2)→(2,1)→(2, 3) \rightarrow (2, 2) \rightarrow (2, 1) \rightarrow(2,3)→(2,2)→(2,1)→ blue lake.
